title: WEBINAR: Why Google Cloud NetApp Volumes Matter for Modern EDA Workloads - Semiwiki
gist: Google Cloud and NetApp are positioning Google Cloud NetApp Volumes as essential infrastructure for modern EDA workloads, which generate hundreds of terabytes of data and require both high-IOPS random access and high-throughput sequential access. The partnership addresses semiconductor design teams' storage bottlenecks, which directly delay tape-out and extend time-to-market. Cloud elasticity allows capacity and performance to scale dynamically with verification and signoff demand spikes rather than requiring costly on-premises overprovisioning.

themes.theme: EDA Storage Demands and Infrastructure Bottlenecks

insights.claim: Modern EDA front-end design and verification workflows generate millions of small files requiring low-latency, high-IOPS storage, while back-end physical verification creates large sequential data streams demanding high throughput — a dual-mode requirement that strains generic storage systems.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Storage vendors that can serve both random-IOPS and sequential-throughput profiles simultaneously hold a structural advantage in the semiconductor EDA market.
insights.quote: Front-end design and verification processes generate millions of small files and metadata operations that require low-latency access and high IOPS performance.

insights.claim: Today's semiconductor design projects can generate hundreds of terabytes of data and involve directory structures containing millions or even billions of files, making storage architecture a first-order engineering constraint rather than a secondary IT concern.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Scale of semiconductor data creates a durable, large-scale storage market opportunity for cloud and enterprise storage vendors with EDA-optimized offerings.
insights.quote: Today's projects can generate hundreds of terabytes of data and involve deeply nested directory structures containing millions or even billions of files.

insights.claim: Storage bottlenecks in EDA environments directly impact engineering productivity, delay project milestones, and extend time-to-market for semiconductor products, creating a direct financial cost that justifies premium cloud storage investment.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Storage performance is now a measurable competitive variable in chip development speed; companies that solve this gain cycle-time advantage over rivals still on-premises.
insights.quote: storage bottlenecks can directly impact engineering productivity, delay project milestones, and extend time-to-market

insights.claim: Production testing and silicon validation workflows generate significant volumes of simulation outputs, test vectors, and manufacturing data that must be stored, managed, and analyzed efficiently, adding a third distinct storage demand profile on top of front-end and back-end EDA needs.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The breadth of storage use cases across the full EDA flow makes multi-protocol, high-performance cloud storage a necessity rather than a convenience for fabless and IDM chip companies.
insights.quote: production testing and silicon validation workflows generate significant volumes of simulation outputs, test vectors, and manufacturing data

insights.claim: Google Cloud has built a comprehensive semiconductor design ecosystem combining high-performance compute instances, GPU accelerators, workload scheduling tools, and cloud-native management capabilities — but positions storage as the missing link that must be solved to realize cloud-based EDA benefits.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Google's framing that compute alone is insufficient positions NetApp Volumes as the enabling layer for Google Cloud's semiconductor vertical, creating a bundled land-and-expand opportunity.
insights.quote: the presenters stress that compute resources alone are not enough. To fully realize the benefits of cloud-based EDA, organizations need storage infrastructure capable of delivering consistent performance at scale.

insights.claim: Google Cloud explicitly targets EDA, HPC, and AI-driven workflows as converging use cases on the same cloud infrastructure, suggesting semiconductor companies moving to cloud will encounter natural upsell from EDA into AI/ML chip design acceleration tools.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Cloud vendors offering unified EDA-plus-AI infrastructure may capture disproportionate semiconductor workloads as AI-assisted chip design grows.
insights.quote: This ecosystem includes high-performance compute instances, GPU accelerators, workload scheduling technologies, and cloud-native management tools designed to support EDA, HPC, and AI-driven workflows.

insights.claim: Semiconductor workloads experience significant fluctuations in resource demand — particularly during verification cycles, tape-out preparation, and signoff — making on-premises infrastructure economically inefficient due to the need to overprovision for peak demand.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The bursty nature of tape-out-driven demand creates a structural TCO argument for cloud-based EDA over fixed on-premises hardware, accelerating semiconductor industry cloud adoption.
insights.quote: Semiconductor workloads often experience significant fluctuations in resource demand, particularly during verification cycles, tape-out preparation, and signoff activities.

insights.claim: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes allows storage capacity and performance to scale alongside compute workloads, avoiding the capital expenditure inefficiency of provisioning on-premises storage for peak tape-out loads that sit idle most of the year.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Elastic storage co-scaling with compute is a key differentiator for cloud EDA economics; investors in on-premises storage for semiconductors face secular demand risk as cloud alternatives mature.
insights.quote: NetApp Volumes allows storage capacity and performance to scale alongside those workloads. This flexibility helps organizations avoid the costs associated with overprovisioning traditional on-premises infrastructure.

themes.theme: Distributed Engineering Teams and Shared IP Access

insights.claim: Semiconductor engineering teams are increasingly distributed across multiple geographic locations and require secure, high-performance access to shared design libraries, IP blocks, and verification results — a requirement that favors cloud-native storage over data-center-local NFS.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Geographically distributed chip design teams represent a structural tailwind for cloud storage vendors with global low-latency delivery; on-premises NAS vendors face displacement risk.
insights.quote: Engineering teams are often distributed across multiple geographic locations and require secure, high-performance access to shared design libraries, intellectual property (IP), and verification results.

insights.claim: NetApp and Google Cloud are co-marketing Google Cloud NetApp Volumes as a foundational infrastructure layer for semiconductor innovation, combining Google's scalable compute with NetApp's enterprise storage to serve the full EDA workflow lifecycle.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The NetApp-Google Cloud partnership deepens both companies' exposure to the growing semiconductor cloud market; success validates NetApp's cloud-first pivot and Google Cloud's vertical industry strategy.
insights.quote: By combining Google Cloud's scalable infrastructure with NetApp's enterprise storage technology, organizations can improve resource utilization, accelerate design cycles, support hybrid cloud strategies, and enable the next generation of EDA workloads.

insights.claim: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes is positioned to support hybrid cloud strategies for semiconductor companies, suggesting NetApp sees a multi-year on-premises-to-cloud migration path rather than an immediate full-cloud transition as the realistic adoption trajectory.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Hybrid cloud positioning lowers switching barriers for semiconductor firms with existing on-premises NAS investments, potentially accelerating NetApp Volumes adoption at the expense of pure-cloud competitors.

insights.claim: Oliver Krause, NetApp's Principal PM for Google Cloud NetApp Volumes, brings 25+ years of storage architecture experience to the product, signaling NetApp is deploying senior enterprise storage expertise to compete in the cloud-native EDA segment.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: NetApp's talent deployment in this product area reflects the strategic priority the company places on winning semiconductor cloud storage market share.
insights.quote: He has over 25 years experience working with large and small companies to architect the right solution for their data storage needs.

themes.theme: Semiconductor Complexity as Structural Storage Demand Driver

insights.claim: As process technologies advance and chip designs become increasingly complex, the storage demands of EDA workflows grow superlinearly, creating a long-duration structural demand tailwind for high-performance cloud storage solutions targeting the semiconductor industry.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Semiconductor complexity trends (multi-die, 3D stacking, advanced nodes) are structural growth drivers for EDA infrastructure spend, making this a durable revenue opportunity for cloud storage vendors.
insights.quote: As process technologies continue to advance and chip designs become increasingly complex, engineering teams require scalable, high-performance infrastructure capable of supporting massive datasets, large compute clusters, and demanding storage requirements.

insights.claim: Storage performance and scalability have become critical factors in semiconductor innovation, elevating storage from a back-office IT cost center to a strategic determinant of chip design competitiveness.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Investors should treat EDA-optimized storage as a strategic product category rather than a commodity; vendors that win this segment capture high-value, sticky enterprise contracts in a growing market.
insights.quote: storage performance and scalability have become critical factors in semiconductor innovation
